after_heading: The MA(q) Model and Its Always-Stationary Property
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: an expressive cartoon character whose facial expression is a smooth blended average of a few small floating shock-bubbles hovering just behind its head, two recent jolts shown as a sharp jagged bubble and a gentle wavy bubble, the character's calm averaged expression visibly mixing those recent moods rather than its own, while older shock-bubbles further back have faded almost to nothing. Style friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. alt: A cartoon character whose calm expression is a blended average of a few recent shock-bubbles floating behind its head while older shocks fade away, dramatizing how a moving-average model carries only a short finite memory of recent random shocks.
caption: A moving-average process wears the blended mood of its last few shocks and nothing older: its memory is finite, which is exactly why it is always stationary.

after_heading: The AR(p) Model: Regression on the Past
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cartoon character speaking into the present while a row of progressively fainter translucent copies of itself trails directly behind it, each ghostly past-self echoing the same posture a beat earlier and fading as it recedes, the front character clearly leaning on its own recent echoes to decide what to do next rather than reacting to anything external. Style friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face and body language, one clear visual idea, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A cartoon character leans forward while a trail of progressively fainter copies of itself echoes behind it, each a past version fading with distance, dramatizing how an autoregressive model predicts its present value as a weighted echo of its own recent past.
caption: Autoregression is a character listening to its own recent echoes: the next value is a weighted reply to the values it just spoke.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: an overeager cartoon model-character proudly tracing a wildly wiggly curve that contorts to touch every single past dot it can see, beaming with misplaced confidence, while just ahead of it the path opens onto blank empty space where its frantic squiggle clearly has no idea what comes next, a tiny calm character beside it raising a skeptical eyebrow. Style friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag of confident-but-wrong, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: An overeager cartoon model proudly threads a wildly wiggly curve through every past data dot while a skeptical companion raises an eyebrow and the path ahead opens onto blank space, dramatizing how a model that fits the training data perfectly can fail completely out of sample.
caption: A model that bends to touch every past point has learned the noise, not the signal: in-sample perfection is the warning sign, not the trophy.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cartoon character standing beside an identical translucent twin of itself drawn one full season older and faintly tinted to look like a past version, the present character lifting away or subtracting the twin like peeling off an overlay, and where the twin is removed a small clean glowing leftover shape remains that is the genuine new change, the repeating seasonal part visibly cancelling out. Style friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face and body language, one clear visual idea of subtracting a yearly twin to reveal what changed, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A cartoon character peels away a faint identical twin of itself from one season earlier, and where the repeating twin is subtracted a small clean glowing leftover remains, picturing how seasonal differencing removes last year's pattern to expose only the genuinely new change.
caption: Seasonal differencing subtracts last year's twin from this year's self, cancelling the repeating pattern so only what genuinely changed is left to model.

alt: A cartoon character walks along a beach leaving footprints where the newest is crisp and each older print fades geometrically faster until the tide washes the oldest away, dramatizing how exponential smoothing weights recent observations heavily and discounts the past geometrically.
caption: Exponential smoothing remembers like footprints in sand: the latest step is vivid, and every older one fades geometrically until the tide takes it.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cartoon judge character holding a simple balance scale, on one pan a heavy compact polished gem representing how well a model fits the data and on the other pan a teetering wobbly tower of too many stacked parameter-blocks representing model complexity, the judge frowning thoughtfully as the overloaded complexity side tips the scale, weighing goodness of fit against the cost of extra parts. alt: A cartoon judge holds a balance scale weighing a heavy polished gem of model fit against a teetering tower of stacked parameter-blocks for complexity, dramatizing how an information criterion rewards a better fit but penalizes a model for every extra parameter it adds.
caption: An information criterion is a judge on a balance: it credits a model for fitting better but charges it for every extra parameter, favoring the leanest good explanation.
